festivals.name: Tết Nguyên Đán
festivals.description: Vietnam's Lunar New Year is the country's most important festival, with fireworks, flower markets, and family reunions.

festivals.name: Mid-Autumn Festival (Tết Trung Thu)
festivals.description: Hoi An's Ancient Town glows entirely by candlelight with lanterns floating on the river during this harvest celebration.

destinations.name: Hanoi
destinations.description: Vietnam's ancient capital layers thousand-year-old Vietnamese culture with French colonial grandeur around Hoan Kiem Lake.

destinations.name: Ha Long Bay
destinations.description: 3,000 limestone karst islands rising from emerald waters, one of Southeast Asia's most iconic seascapes.

destinations.name: Hoi An
destinations.description: A UNESCO ancient trading port where yellow townhouses, silk lanterns, and exceptional local cuisine dazzle visitors.

destinations.name: Ho Chi Minh City
destinations.description: Vietnam's buzzing southern metropolis with War Remnants Museum, Ben Thanh Market, and extraordinary street food.

destinations.name: Sapa
destinations.description: Emerald rice terraces cascade down mist-shrouded mountains near the Chinese border, home to H'mong villages.

destinations.name: Phong Nha-Kẻ Bàng
destinations.description: Home to Son Doong, the world's largest cave, and hundreds of vast cathedral-like caverns in ancient jungle.

destinations.name: Hue
destinations.description: Vietnam's former imperial capital has a UNESCO Citadel, elaborate royal tombs, and its own distinct spicy cuisine.

destinations.name: Ninh Bình
destinations.description: Inland Ha Long Bay: limestone cliffs rise from paddy fields, explored by rowing boat through ancient temples.
